sources: Avoma call 'Zycus Eversana connect', 2025-05-28 (Jeannine Jenkins, Chamika McLean)
metrics.narrative: On the 28 May 2025 call ('Zycus Eversana connect', ~13.4 months ago), Jeannine Jenkins and Chamika McLean quantified the pain in detail: only a 5% automatic PO-to-invoice match rate versus an expected 80% when the incumbent tool was implemented, invoices requiring 4-5 manual touches when PO funds are insufficient, and a dedicated AP inquiry inbox fielding 30-50 vendor emails a day. Jeannine also noted 'our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low,' evidencing executive-level awareness of the cost of the problem. No fresher quantification exists in the evidence read; this remains the most recent data point and it is now over a year old.

champion.narrative: Chamika McLean (AP Manager) was the original point of contact, engaged across two calls (12 Mar 2025 and 18 Mar 2025 intro call), asked for the meeting recording to review and socialize internally, and said she would 'converse with additional individuals in her organization' before scheduling a broader call. She delivered on that by bringing Jeannine Jenkins into the 28 May 2025 session, showing real internal advocacy at the time. However, she has not responded to any outreach since the single confirmed buyer email on 5 Jun 2025 (~13.1 months ago), including the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 attempt (Next_Step__c) to determine whether the initiative is stalled. Champion status has degraded from developing to weak/at-risk.

competition.narrative: The only competitor evidenced is the incumbent Xflow (Microsoft D365 F&O AP module), which Eversana is actively dissatisfied with (5% vs. 80% expected PO-invoice match rate) and is exploring alternatives to replace, per Jeannine Jenkins on 28 May 2025. No named rival vendor has ever surfaced in any call or field. The real live threat is status quo / internal inertia: with no confirmed buyer contact in over a year, the initiative may have stalled, been postponed, or been shelved internally, exactly the question the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 Next Step was meant to resolve.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain was described specifically and repeatedly by Chamika McLean and Jeannine Jenkins on 28 May 2025: the incumbent tool's lack of PO-fund visibility causes invoices to error out at final GL posting, there is no way to split a single invoice across departments against a blanket PO, and significant unapplied cash results because vendor remittance formats don't align with Eversana's AR automation. This is well-evidenced but remains the only pain evidence on file, and it is now roughly 13.4 months old.

sources: Avoma calls 2025-03-12, 2025-03-18, 2025-05-28; no pricing/paper artifacts on record
paper_process.narrative: No commercial, pricing, or contracting conversation has occurred anywhere in the evidence read. The deal has never left the discovery / pain-scoping phase, consistent with its current Qualified stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: Kelly Baker (CFO) is named Decision Maker on the Salesforce contact role, but she has never attended a call or replied to outreach in any evidence read. Her position is referenced only second-hand: on 28 May 2025 Jeannine Jenkins said the CFO 'has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low,' which shows indirect awareness of the pain but not direct engagement with the relevant stakeholder no confirmed EB contact in over a year, this remains an open gap.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process, timeline, or internal approval chain has been discussed in any call or field on record. The only forward step ever proposed was scheduling a live demo/workshop with a solution consultant (28 May 2025), which itself was never delivered, so how Eversana would actually move from evaluation to a decision is unknown.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated a threshold of wanting a replacement system that meets 'at least 80% of requirements' (Jeannine Jenkins, 28 May 2025) versus the incumbent's current 5% automatic match rate, and asked pointed questions about D365 integration effort and IT resource needs. No formal RFP, scoring framework, or written requirements list has surfaced since.

items.name: Sequence call attempt to Matt Goyer
items.summary: Automated step 10 of the Emerging Enterprise outbound cadence (via Nooks dialer) placed a call to Matt Goyer; no notes or connect outcome recorded, so it's unclear if contact was made — still awaiting a live response.

day_summary.overall: A single dialer task fired today as part of the Emerging Enterprise outbound sequence, with a call attempt to Matt Goyer at Eversana marking step 10 of that cadence. No outcome, notes, or callback details were logged, so this reads as a routine automated touchpoint rather than a substantive conversation — the deal remains stalled at the prospecting/outreach stage, waiting on Matt to actually engage.

cro_panel.intro: A data-rich but badly stalled early-stage deal; a strong, quantified pain case was built in one deep-dive call over a year ago, but there has been no confirmed buyer engagement since, no executive access, and a close date that is no longer realistic

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.detail: 407 days dark on every channel (meetings, email, SFDC touches at 0 in 30/60/90d); the rep's own Next Step openly asks whether the initiative is stalled, postponed, or cancelled.

Close date already pushed 83 days (Apr'26 → Jul'26) while the deal sits in early-stage Qualified with no demo/pricing/contracting started — a clear stage-vs-timeline mismatch.
No named competitor, but sustained buyer silence for over a year makes status-quo/do-nothing the dominant threat — the deal may already be internally shelved.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved quickly through two discovery-style calls in March 2025 and a detailed pain-discovery call in late May 2025, then went essentially silent: one confirmed buyer email reply on 5 Jun 2025, a rep note in mid-Oct 2025 flagging the need to check if the initiative had stalled, and an unclear Salesforce activity touch in early May 2026, with no confirmed buyer engagement content since.

forecast_read.math: days_to_close = 13 (CloseDate 2026-07-22 vs today 2026-07-09). Stage is Qualified, which per the enterprise motion still requires demo -> RFI/RFP -> shortlist -> commercials -> negotiation -> contracting before close; none of these have begun. Last confirmed two-way buyer touch (incoming email) was 2025-06-05, 399 days (~13.1 months) ago.
forecast_read.reason: A close date of 22 Jul 2026, only 13 days from today (9 Jul 2026), is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal that has never had a demo, pricing conversation, or contracting motion, and has had no confirmed buyer contact since 5 Jun 2025.
forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline stays, but the Close Date needs to move out substantially (or the opportunity should be re-qualified / flagged at risk) given roughly 13 months of buyer silence.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-03-18, moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 2025-03-21 (476 days ago, still in Qualified 476 days later). Close date has been pushed four times (18 Sep 2025 -> 20 Feb 2026 -> 6 Feb 2026 -> 30 Apr 2026 -> 22 Jul 2026, last push 14 Apr 2026) with zero corresponding stage movement. Today is 15 days from the current close date with no evaluation milestone (demo, pricing, contract) yet reached.
evidence: Next step still reads '...determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled' (SF Next_Step__c, dated 2025-10-13)
north_star_verdict.headline: The Eversana AP-automation deal has been effectively dormant for over a year: the last genuine two-way buyer contact was Chamika McLean's 5 Jun 2025 email, and 13 months of steady rep outreach (calls, emails) has drawn no reply since. The 22 Jul 2026 close date (15 days out as of this sweep) is not credible against a deal still sitting in Qualified stage with no demo, pricing, or contracting conversation ever begun.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pull out of the current quarter / push close date materially; no realistic path to close by 22 Jul 2026 given the deal remains in Qualified stage with no live buyer engagement, demo, or pricing conversation on record.

flags: Re-open the thread with Chamika McLean directly — the 5%-match Xflow pain and CFO-level frustration she described on 28 May 2025 remain unresolved and Zycus has a strong technical fit; personal re-engagement is the highest-leverage move available.

Multi-thread beyond Chamika: bring in Matt Goyer (VP Procurement) or Justin Stenz (SVP Finance) as a second point of contact, since the deal currently rests on one AP-side champion who has gone quiet for over a year.
Re-engage CFO Kelly Baker directly instead of routing only through Chamika McLean; Jeannine Jenkins already confirmed CFO-level frustration with AP throughput but Kelly Baker has never joined a call.
Convert the 28 May 2025 pain data (5% vs 80% match rate, 4-5 touches per invoice, CFO frustration) into a written one-page business case now, before another sweep passes with zero fresh evidence.

Treat the 13-month silence as a re-engagement problem, not a nurture problem: escalate past Chamika McLean to Jeannine Jenkins or Justin Stenz with a fresh, dated ROI hook tied to their own stated 80% match-rate target.
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage move is resolving the rep's own unanswered question from 9 months ago: is this initiative still alive. Everything else (multi-threading, EB access, delivering the promised demo) is blocked until that is known.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus vs. any alternative has been recorded in any call; the only stated preference-adjacent fact is dissatisfaction with the incumbent tool, Xflow, expressed by Jeannine Jenkins on 2025-05-28.

competitive_position.summary: No named rival vendor has ever surfaced. The two real competitive forces are the incumbent AP tool the buyer is actively unhappy with, and internal inertia: with the buying team silent for over a year, the initiative may have quietly stalled or been shelved.

hard.ais_why:
• Two recorded discovery calls show clear interest in AI-driven AP automation; Zycus pitched cognitive OCR and AI invoice processing to fix Eversana's failing Xflow tool (5% PO-match versus 80% expected).
• Eversana's stakeholder explicitly framed AI as pivotal for innovation and reducing manual work, asking pointed questions about Microsoft Dynamics integration and IT effort needed for an AI procurement rollout.
• Qualified stage with two substantive 2025 calls; opportunity has stalled into nurture mode (Oct 13 next step questions if initiative is cancelled), limiting confidence despite strong AI receptiveness.
hard.next_step: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo (internal AP process inertia / initiative shelved)
